items: Create & track woodwork orders (furniture, repairs, custom designs)
Order status tracking (Pending  In Progress  Completed  Delivered)
Track raw materials (wood types, nails, polish, paint, tools)
Auto-decrease stock when assigned to an order

srs.path: FYP Doc Final\_extracted\Software Requirements Specification Doc.md

headings.title: Phases of the VU Process Model
Reasons for Selecting the VU Process Model
Work Plan (Use MS Project to create Schedule/Work Plan)

fields.Title: Register Employee
fields.Description: Admin registers a new employee in the system by providing required details and login credentials.
fields.Pre-Condition: Admin is logged into the system.
fields.Post-Condition: Employee account is created and stored in the system with login credentials.
fields.Main Flow:
1. Admin opens employee registration form.
2. Admin enters employee details (name, email, role, employee type, hourly rate).
3. Admin sets or system generates a password.
4. Admin submits the form.
5. System validates input data.
6. System checks for duplicate email.
7. System saves employee record.
fields.Alternative Flow:
A1: Missing or invalid fields  System displays validation error.
A2: Duplicate email  System shows warning and prevents saving.
fields.Exceptions: E1: Database failure  System displays error message.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Register Customer
fields.Description: Admin registers a new customer with contact details.
fields.Pre-Condition: Admin is logged into the system.
fields.Post-Condition: Customer details are successfully stored in the system.
fields.Main Flow:
1. Admin opens customer registration form.
2. Admin enters customer details (name, phone, email, address).
3. Admin submits the form.
4. System validates input data.
5. System checks for duplicate customer (phone/email).
6. System saves customer record.
fields.Alternative Flow:
A1: Missing or invalid fields  System displays validation error.
A2: Duplicate customer detected  System shows warning and prevents saving.
fields.Exceptions: E1: Database failure  System displays error message.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Create Order
fields.Description: Admin creates new order for furniture/custom work.
fields.Post-Condition: Order is saved with Pending status.
fields.Main Flow:
1. Admin selects customer.
2. Admin enters order details (type, description, quantity).
3. Admin selects required materials.
4. System triggers UC15 (Cost Estimation).
5. System displays estimated cost.
6. Admin reviews and confirms the order.
7. System creates the order with Pending status.
fields.Alternative Flow: A1: Required materials unavailable  System shows warning and prevents order creation.
fields.Exceptions: E1: Stock data missing  System displays error and stops process.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Track Order Status
fields.Description: Admin and employee view the current status and progress of an order.
fields.Post-Condition: Order status and progress details are displayed.
fields.Main Flow:
1. User selects an order.
2. System retrieves order details.
3. System displays current status (Pending  In Progress  Completed  Delivered).
4. System displays progress history (if available).
fields.Alternative Flow: A1: No progress updates available  System displays current status only.
fields.Exceptions: E1: Order not found  System displays error message.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Manage Raw Materials
fields.Description: Admin manages inventory items including adding, updating, and deleting raw materials along with their associated details.
fields.Pre-Condition: Admin is logged into the system.
fields.Post-Condition: Material data is successfully updated and stored in the system.
fields.Main Flow:
1. Admin opens inventory management module.
2. System displays list of available materials.
3. Admin adds a new material or selects an existing material.
4. Admin enters or updates material details (name, quantity, unit price, supplier, threshold level).
5. Admin confirms the operation (add/update/delete).
6. System validates input data.
7. System saves changes to the database.
fields.Alternative Flow:
A1: Invalid input (e.g., negative quantity, missing required fields)  System displays validation error and prevents saving.
A2: Duplicate material entry  System shows warning and prevents duplication.
fields.Exceptions:
E1: Material record not found  System displays error message.
E2: Database error  System displays error and stops operation.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Assign Worker to Job
fields.Description: Admin assigns one or more employees (workers/craftsmen) to a specific order.
fields.Pre-Condition: Worker exists and is available; order exists in the system.
fields.Post-Condition: Worker(s) successfully assigned to the order.
fields.Main Flow:
1. Admin opens order details.
2. System displays list of available workers.
3. Admin selects one or more workers.
4. Admin confirms assignment.
5. System validates worker availability.
6. System assigns worker(s) to the order and saves changes.
fields.Alternative Flow:
A1: Worker already assigned  System displays warning.
A2: Selected worker unavailable  System prevents assignment and shows message.
fields.Exceptions: E1: Worker record not found  System displays error message.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Generate Invoice
fields.Description: Admin generates an invoice for a completed order.
fields.Post-Condition: Invoice is successfully created and stored in the system.
fields.Main Flow:
1. Admin opens completed order.
2. Admin requests invoice generation.
3. System retrieves final cost (materials + labor).
4. System displays invoice details.
5. Admin reviews and confirms.
6. System generates and saves the invoice.
fields.Alternative Flow: A1: Invoice already generated  System displays warning and prevents duplication.
fields.Exceptions: E1: Cost data missing  System displays error message and stops process.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Record Payment
fields.Description: Admin records partial or full payment for a customer invoice.
fields.Post-Condition: Payment is recorded and invoice balance is updated.
fields.Main Flow:
1. Admin opens invoice details.
2. System displays invoice amount and remaining balance.
3. Admin clicks Add Payment.
4. Admin enters payment amount.
5. System validates payment amount.
6. System records payment and updates remaining balance.
7. System displays updated payment status.
fields.Alternative Flow:
A1: Payment amount exceeds due  System displays error and prevents saving.
A2: Invalid amount ( 0)  System displays validation error.
fields.Exceptions: E1: Database failure  System displays error message.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Track Employee Performance
fields.Description: Admin reviews performance metrics of employees based on completed tasks and work records.
fields.Pre-Condition: Employee records and completed task data exist in the system.
fields.Main Flow:
1. Admin selects an employee.
2. Admin selects a time range (e.g., monthly or custom).
3. System retrieves employee task and work history.
4. System calculates performance metrics (tasks completed, hours worked, efficiency).
5. System displays performance report.
fields.Alternative Flow: A1: No records found  System displays No performance data available.
fields.Exceptions: E1: Error in fetching data  System displays error message.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Generate Reports & Analytics
fields.Description: Admin generates financial and operational reports based on system data.
fields.Pre-Condition: Relevant data exists in the system.
fields.Post-Condition: Report is displayed and optionally exported.
fields.Main Flow:
1. Admin opens Reports module.
2. Admin selects report type (financial, material usage, employee performance, order timeline).
3. Admin selects date range or filters.
4. System retrieves relevant data.
5. System generates report.
6. System displays report and provides export option (e.g., PDF/CSV).
fields.Alternative Flow: A1: Invalid date range  System displays validation error and prevents report generation.
fields.Exceptions: E1: System timeout or data retrieval error  System displays error message.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Update Task Progress
fields.Description: Employee updates the progress status of assigned tasks within an order.
fields.Pre-Condition: Employee is assigned to the order.
fields.Post-Condition: Order progress is updated and recorded in the system.
fields.Main Flow:
1. Employee opens assigned order.
2. System displays current order status.
3. Employee selects next progress stage.
4. Employee submits update.
5. System validates allowed status transition.
6. System updates order status and logs progress change.
7. System displays confirmation of successful update.
fields.Alternative Flow:
A1: Invalid status transition  System rejects update and displays error.
A2: Employee selects same status  System shows warning and prevents update.
fields.Exceptions: E1: Order not assigned to employee  System denies access and displays error message.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Track Work Hours & Wages
fields.Description: Employee records work hours for assigned tasks, and the system calculates wages based on approved hours.
fields.Pre-Condition: Employee is assigned to a job.
fields.Post-Condition: Work hours are recorded and wages are calculated and updated.
fields.Main Flow:
1. Employee opens assigned task.
2. Employee enters work hours.
3. System validates entered hours.
4. Admin reviews submitted hours.
5. Admin approves or modifies hours.
6. System calculates wages using predefined rates.
7. System updates wage record and displays confirmation.
fields.Alternative Flow:
A1: Employee does not enter hours  No update performed.
A2: Admin rejects hours  System notifies employee and does not update wages.
fields.Exceptions: E1: Invalid hours (negative, zero, or excessively large)  System displays validation error.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Auto-Decrease Stock
fields.Description: System automatically deducts material quantity when materials are assigned during order creation.
fields.Pre-Condition: Materials are selected and assigned to an order.
fields.Main Flow:
1. System receives material assignment from order creation.
2. System checks availability of required materials.
3. System validates sufficient stock quantity.
4. System deducts allocated quantity from inventory.
5. System updates stock levels in the database.
fields.Alternative Flow: A1: Insufficient stock  System displays warning and prevents order confirmation.
fields.Exceptions: E1: Database error  System displays error message and stops operation.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Low-Stock Alert
fields.Description: System notifies the admin when material stock falls below a defined threshold.
fields.Pre-Condition: Stock update occurs in the system.
fields.Post-Condition: Low-stock notification is generated and displayed to the admin.
fields.Main Flow:
1. System updates inventory after stock change.
2. System compares current stock with predefined threshold.
3. If stock is below threshold, system generates alert.
4. System displays notification to admin.
fields.Alternative Flow: A1: Stock level above threshold  No alert generated.
fields.Exceptions: E1: Notification system failure  System logs error and displays message.
fields.Author: BC210202812

fields.Title: Generate Cost Estimation
fields.Description: System calculates estimated cost of an order based on selected materials and labor charges.
fields.Pre-Condition: Materials and labor details are selected for the order.
fields.Post-Condition: Estimated cost is calculated and displayed.
fields.Main Flow:
1. Admin selects materials and labor details during order creation.
2. System fetches material prices.
3. System calculates total material cost.
4. System calculates labor cost based on predefined rates.
5. System combines both values to generate total estimated cost.
6. System displays estimated cost to the admin.
fields.Alternative Flow: A1: Required data missing  System displays error and prevents estimation.
fields.Exceptions: E1: Data retrieval error  System displays error message.
fields.Author: BC210202812

headings.title: The class diagram represents the structural design of the Woodcraft Management System. It illustrates the key domain entities, their attributes, and relationships, providing a clear overview of how data is organized and connected within the system.
